title: Emulator — Network
description: Inter-tier network behaviour per node-to-node link: latency (configured one-way delay vs measured one-way latency) and bandwidth (measured throughput per link). Latency values are one-way (the measured TCP round-trip halved, since the inject is symmetric) so they line up with the configured one-way delay; throughput is the measured iperf3 egress summed across the source role's pods.

list.name: base_ms
list.label: Base one-way RTT to subtract (ms)
list.description: Unshaped network latency between nodes, subtracted from the measured value so the latency panels track the configured inject. ~half the node-to-node round-trip base (≈3ms here). Set 0 to see the raw measurement.

panels.title: Configured latency between nodes
panels.description: One-way latency configured in the template for each tier pair (e.g. edge -> fog = 10ms means 10ms A→B and 10ms B→A). Updates immediately on PATCH.

panels.title: Measured one-way latency between nodes — min / avg / max (selected period)
panels.description: One row per node-to-node link (source tier → destination tier), averaged across the source pods, over the selected time range — one-way (the measured TCP round-trip halved; valid because the injected delay is symmetric). Min ≈ the configured one-way delay — compare it directly against the configured panel above. Avg/Max sit higher because the netem timer slips under VM scheduling jitter and queuing, so they overstate the true link delay. The unshaped base RTT is subtracted via the `base_ms` control at the top (set it to 0 to see the raw measurement); any remainder above the configured value is real queuing under load.

panels.title: One-way latency between nodes (time series)
panels.description: Measured one-way latency for each node-to-node link (source tier → destination tier), averaged across the source pods, over the selected time range — set the range with the dashboard time picker (top-right) to see the last X minutes. TCP round-trip halved, probed every 15s; tracks the injected link delay (tc netem) plus real network jitter. The unshaped base RTT is subtracted via the `base_ms` control at the top (set it to 0 for the raw measurement).

panels.title: Configured bandwidth between nodes
panels.description: Bandwidth cap configured in the template for each tier pair (e.g. edge -> fog = 1000mbps means the tc rate limit applied on the link in each direction). Updates immediately on PATCH. The throughput panels below should sit at or under these caps.

panels.title: Throughput between nodes (time series)
panels.description: Measured egress throughput for each node-to-node link (source tier → destination tier), summed across the source role's pods, from worker_peer_egress_mbps (parsed from the iperf3 client's per-second interval reports). This is the actual traffic crossing the link; compare it against the configured bandwidth cap above (which tc enforces on the tier node's NIC). Values are converted to bits/sec so Grafana auto-scales to Mb/s.

panels.title: Measured throughput between nodes — min / avg / max (selected period)
panels.description: One row per node-to-node link (source tier → destination tier), summed across the source role's pods, over the selected time range. Avg is the steady-state throughput on the link; Max approaches the configured `bandwidth` cap when the link saturates. Values are bits/sec (auto-scaled to Mb/s).
